name: Cranberry Honey Mustard
recipeIngredient: 1 1/4 cups Dried cranberries (available in health-food and gourmet)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a non-aluminum pot or jar, combine the mustard seeds, cider vinegar, and dried cranberries; cover and soak for 48 hours, adding additional vinegar if necessary to maintain enough liquid to cover the seeds.
2. Scrape the soaked seed-and-cranberry mixture into a food processor.
3. Process until the mixture turns from liquid and seeds to a creamy mixture flecked with seeds and bits of cranberry. Add honey and salt.
4. The process takes at least 3-4 minutes, so be patient. You may need to add additional vinegar as necessary to create a nice creamy mustard; keep in mind that it will thicken slightly upon standing.
5. After about one week of aging, the cranberry flavor seems to settle into this mustard and make it all the better, but it is perfectly good immediately. Makes about 3 1/3 cups.
